instruction: Leave the bathroom and take a right. Walk down the hallway into the entry way and stop next to the console table on the right, and the banister to the stairs on the right.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Leave the bathroom and take a right. Walk down the hallway into the entry way and stop next to the console table on the right, and the banister to the stairs on the right. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
Let me get a full view of my surroundings to understand the layout.

Behind me is the bathroom (I can see tile and cabinets). The instruction is to leave the bathroom and take a right. Ahead of me there's a hallway/doorway. To my right (+90°) I see a room with a hallway leading to what looks like an entry area with lighter flooring.

The bathroom is behind me. So "leaving the bathroom" means moving forward/ahead. Then take a right. Let me move ahead through the doorway first.
I'm very close to a wall now. Let me observe.

I've moved into a tight spot. This appears to be a doorway/mirror. I think I misjudged. Let me reconsider — the bathroom was behind me, and the room to the right (+90°) had the entry way with the console table. Let me back up and look around again.

Now I can see a hallway with dark wood flooring and framed pictures. There's a French door on the right and an open area ahead. This looks like the hallway leading toward the entry. Let me turn a bit more right to face down the hallway clearly and move forward.

Now I can see the hallway more clearly. There's a French door on the left, framed pictures, and to the right I can see what appears to be an entry area with stairs (a banister visible on the right). Let me move forward down the hallway toward the entry.
I see the French door is directly ahead. The entry area with stairs is to the right. I need to go toward the entry way. Let me turn right to head into the open area where I saw the stairs.

Now I can see through the doorway into the entry area. There's a console table with items on it (the dark table in the center-right), and I can see stairs with a banister in the background. This is where I need to go. Let me move forward through this doorway into the entry.

Now I'm entering the entry way. I can see the console table on the right (dark long table against the wall) and stairs with a banister on the right. Let me move forward into the entry area and get closer.

I'm now in the entry way. I can see the banister to the stairs on the right (the dark railing in the lower right). The console table appears to be on the right side too. Let me look around to locate the console table precisely.

Now I have a clear picture:
- **Right (+90°)**: The banister/railing to the stairs is clearly on my right.
- **Behind (+180°)**: I can see the hallway leading back to the bathroom.
- **Ahead**: The entry way with stairs and a console table area.

The console table with the vase/flowers is in the ahead view (center-left). The banister is on my right. I'm in the entry way now with the banister on my right. 

Let me move forward a bit more to get right next to the console table and banister. The console table with flowers appears ahead-left. Let me get closer to be within 3m of both.

Now I'm well into the entry way. I can see:
- The console table with a vase/flowers ahead-left (against the wall)
- The banister to the stairs on the lower-right (the dark wooden railing)
- The stairs are visible on the left

I'm now positioned in the entry way with the banister on my right. The console table is ahead-left. Let me check — the instruction says "stop next to the console table on the right, and the banister on the right." 

The banister here is on the right (lower right). The console table with flowers seems to be more ahead-left. Let me move forward a touch and slightly toward the console table to be next to both. Let me move forward a couple more steps.

Now I can see clearly:
- The console table with vase/flowers is ahead-left (against the wall, with decorative vases below)
- The banister to the stairs is in the lower-right (dark wooden railing visible)
- The stairs are on the left with a banister

I'm now standing in the entry way. The console table is close (about 2-3m ahead-left) and the banister is close on the right. I'm right next to both. 

This matches the instruction: "stop next to the console table on the right, and the banister to the stairs on the right." I'm within 3 meters of both landmarks now. Let me issue STOP.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 78 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 22, "tool_calls_remaining": 78}

text: Let me verify my placement with a look around before committing.

Confirming my position:
- **Right (+90°)**: The banister to the stairs is clearly on my right (dark wooden railing going down the stairs). ✓
- **Behind (+180°)**: The hallway leading back to the bathroom. ✓
- **Ahead**: The console table with vase and decorative pieces ahead-left against the wall. ✓

I'm in the entry way with the banister to the stairs on the right, and the console table nearby. This matches the instruction endpoint perfectly. I'm within 3 meters of both landmarks. Let me confirm STOP.
